signals.title: EUR/USD — Trump Auto Tariff Escalation
signals.text: Trump's announcement of a 25% tariff on European autos drives immediate downside pressure on EUR/USD from its 1.1723 Friday close. This trade protectionism reduces European export competitiveness and outweighs the current EUR COT short position of -18.3k which already reflects bearish sentiment. The next ECB meeting on 30 April is the catalyst for assessing the central bank's response to these trade-induced growth risks.
evidence: Trump: Tariffs on European cars and trucks to increase to 25%

signals.title: USD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Intervention Risk
signals.text: JPY's 1W momentum of +1.08% runs against the 300bp USD-JPY carry advantage following confirmed $34.5 billion yen intervention. The JPY COT net short of -88.5k creates significant squeeze risk if the pair breaks below the 157.03 Friday close as speculators are forced to cover. The next BoJ meeting is the catalyst for determining if a rate hike will support the intervention-led JPY recovery.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Crowded Long Unwind Risk
signals.text: AUD COT LF net long +48.3k (74.8% long) creates a crowded-long unwind risk despite the RBA's 4.10% rate and hike bias. While retail sentiment is 90% short, the institutional long exposure is vulnerable to a risk-off shift if US-Iran peace talks fail or oil prices spike. The next RBA meeting on 5 May is the catalyst for confirming the hawkish path required to sustain these long positions.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Positioning Overstretch vs Geopolitical Risk
signals.text: GBP COT net long +32.6k (67.3% long) faces downside risk as US-Iran tensions and failed deal proposals drive a flight to the USD. The BoE's active hold at 3.75% limits further upside momentum for the pair which closed Friday at 1.3575. The next BoE meeting on 30 April is the catalyst for resolving whether the central bank will pivot toward June easing.
evidence: Trump: Not satisfied with latest Iran proposal

signals.title: USD/CAD — Oil Price Compression
signals.text: WTI crude's 2.45% decline to $102.5 weighs on the Loonie, supporting USD/CAD upside from the 1.3586 level. CAD COT net short of -54.7k reinforces the bearish bias as the 150bp carry advantage in favor of the USD persists. The next BoC meeting on 29 April is the catalyst for determining if the central bank will address the impact of volatile energy prices on the inflation outlook.
